profile.name: Sungmin Eum
profile.title:
Research Scientist/PI at [US Army Research Laboratory (ARL)](https://arl.devcom.army.mil/)
 Adjunct Faculty at the [University of Maryland, College Park](https://umd.edu/)
profile.tagline: Computer vision, machine learning, robotics, and language-grounded AI with an emphasis on models that solve complex tasks with limited supervision.
profile.email: sungmin dot eum dot civ at army dot mil | smeum at umd dot edu
profile.affiliation: U.S. Army Research Laboratory · University of Maryland

featured.label: Teaches
featured.value: Large Language Models and Deep Learning for Computer Vision

sections.about:
I am a research scientist and a principal investigator (PI) at the [US Army Research Laboratory](https://arl.devcom.army.mil/). I received my Ph.D. from the [University of Maryland, College Park](https://umd.edu/) in 2017 under the supervision of [David Doermann](https://engineering.buffalo.edu/computer-science-engineering/people/faculty-directory.host.html/content/shared/engineering/computer-science-engineering/profiles/faculty/ladder/doermann-david.html), after earning my B.S. and M.S. from [Yonsei University](https://www.yonsei.ac.kr/en_sc/index.do). My work has also included research internships at [Palo Alto Research Center (PARC)](https://www.sri.com/research/future-concepts-division/) (Now, [Stanford Research Institute (SRI)](https://www.sri.com/)) and [ARL](https://arl.devcom.army.mil/).

I also serve as an adjunct faculty in the [ECE](https://ece.umd.edu/) Department & [MAGE](https://mage.umd.edu/) at the [University of Maryland, College Park](https://umd.edu/), where I teach graduate/undergraduate courses: Large Language Models (LLMs), Deep Learning for Computer Vision.
sections.research: My research spans computer vision, machine learning, artificial intelligence, robotics, and natural language processing. I study how intelligent systems learn, represent, and reason from visual and language signals, particularly under limited supervision. More recently, I have been exploring machine unlearning, the ability to make AI models selectively forget learned knowledge, to better understand where knowledge resides within learned representations and how it can be modified.

news.text: Attending IROS 2024 in Abu Dhabi and presenting an oral paper on leveraging depth during training for unsupervised obstacle segmentation.
SynPlay dataset paper posted on arXiv with an accompanying project page.
Attended AAAI 2023 in Washington, DC and presented work at the Creative AI Across Modalities workshop.
Paper accepted to ECCV on hard-distance elastic loss for person re-identification.
ME R-CNN was featured in the Army Top 10 Advances of 2020.

gallery.intro: A small private room for visual studies, travel fragments, and image sequences that sit adjacent to the research work.

collections.title: Paul Before the Earthquake
collections.description: The composition portrays the stillness preceding one of the New Testament's most remarkable prison scenes. Bound in chains yet at peace, Paul is shown not in anticipation of deliverance but in quiet devotion. The work invites reflection on the contrast between outward confinement and inward freedom. Inspired by Acts 16.

collections.title: The Interval
collections.description: Between the breath before the first note and the breath after the last lies a fleeting moment of quiet tension. This work lingers there, where anticipation becomes memory, and where silence speaks with the same quiet presence as the music itself. Inspired by my wife, J.
